title: Persona / assessment profile
description: A golden persona or a real assessment profile: sovereignty priorities plus one scenario per workload tier. Drafted by Claude Code with status: draft; only the project owner may set status: approved (persona ownership workflow).

summary.description: What this persona is designed to exercise, e.g. 'facility-sovereign but tech/ops-constrained — must NOT score as sovereign across the board'.

priorities.description: Government's sovereignty outcome ranking, most important first. Domain weights derive from this ranking (design principle 5), not fixed ECSF procurement weights.

classification_scheme.description: Whether this persona uses the default 4-tier scheme or a national classification (named in tiers[].tier_id).

tiers.description: One entry per workload tier assessed for this persona. classification tiering (design principle 6): one government = several assessments = a tiered posture map.

tier_id.description: e.g. public, internal, restricted, secret, or a national classification label.

axis_b.description: Platform stack. Applies mainly to A1/A2; omit where not applicable (design has no such party/layer for A3-A5 upper stack).

provider_jurisdiction.description: Legal home jurisdiction of the provider, expressed relative to {NATION}/{TRUSTED_REGION}.

facility_jurisdiction.description: Jurisdiction of the physical facility, expressed relative to {NATION}/{TRUSTED_REGION}.

extraterritorial_law_exposure.description: Whether a foreign extraterritorial law (e.g. CLOUD Act analog) can reach this deployment.

provider_stack.description: Owner-review addition (D-025): the underlying platform stack beneath a third-party provider (e.g. Axis A3's local CSP), when it differs from the provider entity/facility jurisdiction — same value set as axis_b since it's the same kind of stack distinction, just one layer down the supply chain.
